reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff (May 30, 2025), two out of ten New Orleans jail escapees (Antoine Massey and Derrick Groves) remain at large, 14 days after the May 16 jailbreak. The timeline gives authorities exactly one month (until July 1) to capture or kill both men for a YES resolution. Factors favoring their capture include a massive multi-agency manhunt, the arrest of at least 13 accomplices (which severs their logistics and support networks), and a recently increased $50,000 reward each, which strongly incentivizes tips. However, Massey's prior history of escaping and evading capture, the fact they have eluded police for two weeks, and the possibility they have crossed state lines (as two other escapees were caught in Texas) present non-trivial tail risks that they could disappear for longer than 45 total days. Assuming an 80-85% chance for each to be caught in the remaining timeframe, the joint probability falls around 65-70%. I estimate a 68% chance that both are caught or killed before July 1.
